decision.statement: What should be the canonical planning surface and remote trigger model for autonomous AI development loops?
decision.description: The system should keep planning durable and reviewable, allow remote autonomous execution, feed real validation back into the loop through tests and GitHub Actions, and preserve provider flexibility so lower-cost models such as GLM can handle longer unsupervised work.

options.label: GitHub Issues Primary
options.description: Store work definitions in issues and comments, and trigger runs directly from issue comments or labels.

options.label: Roadmap Canonical + GitHub Triggers
options.description: Keep work units in roadmap files, then trigger runs from GitHub by referencing a roadmap work unit.

options.label: External PM Primary
options.description: Use another project-management system for the canonical plan and sync enough state into GitHub for execution.

options.label: PR-Only Control Plane
options.description: Use pull requests and PR events as the main place to start, iterate on, and validate autonomous work.

GitHub Issues Primary.text: Planning lives outside the repo and is not versioned with code.

Roadmap Canonical + GitHub Triggers.text: Work units live in the repo and are versioned with implementation history.

External PM Primary.text: Usually outside the repo and depends on separate backups and export discipline.

PR-Only Control Plane.text: Some execution history is versioned, but planning only exists after work has already started.

GitHub Issues Primary.text: Very convenient because comments and labels are already the operator surface.

Roadmap Canonical + GitHub Triggers.text: Good if GitHub comments or CLI commands trigger runs by roadmap slug or id.

External PM Primary.text: Usually requires sync glue or secondary GitHub triggers.

PR-Only Control Plane.text: Works after a PR exists, but is awkward as the first step.

GitHub Issues Primary.text: Can connect well to Actions, but planning and validation context can drift apart.

Roadmap Canonical + GitHub Triggers.text: Strong fit: roadmap defines scope, GitHub runs tests, PRs, and checks feed results back.

External PM Primary.text: Validation stays in GitHub, but planning context is one system removed.

PR-Only Control Plane.text: Excellent for validation once code exists in a branch or PR.

GitHub Issues Primary.text: Compatible with OpenCode and custom providers if the workflow is designed that way.

Roadmap Canonical + GitHub Triggers.text: Best fit for a provider-agnostic execution layer with OpenCode plus custom model routing.

External PM Primary.text: Possible, but extra integration layers add friction.

PR-Only Control Plane.text: Often pushes toward vendor-specific PR agents unless designed carefully.

GitHub Issues Primary.text: Discussion and runs are visible, but canonical scope can drift in long issue threads.

Roadmap Canonical + GitHub Triggers.text: Scope is versioned in repo and runs are visible in GitHub history, checks, and PRs.

External PM Primary.text: Audit trail is split across tools and harder to reconstruct.

PR-Only Control Plane.text: Execution history is clear, but pre-PR planning decisions are weaker.

GitHub Issues Primary.text: Very familiar and integrated with current habits.

Roadmap Canonical + GitHub Triggers.text: Mostly familiar if GitHub remains the operator surface and roadmap stays repo-local.

External PM Primary.text: Depends on adopting another planning system and workflow.

PR-Only Control Plane.text: Familiar for code review and CI flows.

GitHub Issues Primary.text: Low implementation cost because GitHub comments and labels already exist.

Roadmap Canonical + GitHub Triggers.text: Moderate cost: add a roadmap selector or slug resolver, but no major platform dependency.

External PM Primary.text: Highest cost because it requires sync, identity, and drift management.

PR-Only Control Plane.text: Moderate cost if limited to PR automation, but it does not solve planning cleanly.

GitHub Issues Primary.text: Possible, but ad hoc issue prompts make it easier for runs to sprawl.

Roadmap Canonical + GitHub Triggers.text: Best fit because scoped roadmap work units constrain runs and preserve cheap-model defaults.

External PM Primary.text: Depends on sync quality and process discipline rather than repo-local constraints.

PR-Only Control Plane.text: Validation costs are clear, but initiation still tends to be heavier and later in the loop.

GitHub Issues Primary.text: Good operator UX, but not strong enough as the canonical planning store.

Roadmap Canonical + GitHub Triggers.text: Recommended: keep roadmap files canonical and trigger execution from GitHub using a roadmap slug, id, or path.

External PM Primary.text: Not recommended unless an external stakeholder workflow becomes mandatory.

PR-Only Control Plane.text: Useful as a validation stage, but not sufficient as the main control plane.
